reviewer.findings: C3 makes red() (the canonical mod-Q residue used by every reduction on the live NTT path: ntt_fwd/inv_kem, basemul_kem, plus decaps) branch-free: `r + ((r >> 31) & Q)` instead of `if r < 0 { r + Q } else { r }`. Purely arithmetic rewrite, no algorithmic change. All 12 pq_kem tests pass (cargo test -p bebop2-core --lib pq_kem::), including kem_golden_vectors_frozen, confirming bit-identical output to the branching form.

overlap.findings: Verified the branchless identity by construction: x mod Q via Rust's `%` on i64 yields r in (-Q, Q) since |x| is bounded; r>>31 is an arithmetic shift on i32 that is all-1s (-1) iff r<0 and 0 otherwise, so `r + ((r>>31) & Q)` adds Q exactly when r is negative, matching the branching form for every r in (-Q, Q). This motivates C4 (the dudect gate on this exact function): a debug/-O0 build previously kept a real conditional branch here that leaked timing; the release cmov was already constant-time, but branch-free-by-construction removes the optimization-level dependency entirely.
